ModMetadata.Name: Spray Paint Plus
ModMetadata.Author: SixFive7
ModMetadata.Description:
[h1]Spray Paint Plus[/h1]
Combines [b]color cycling[/b], [b]network painting[/b], [b]glow paint[/b], and [b]infinite spray paint[/b] into one multiplayer-safe Stationeers mod. See the Credits section below for attribution to the original mods this one builds on.
Full multiplayer compatibility. Safe to remove from existing savegames.
[h2]Features[/h2]
[h3]Full Multiplayer Support[/h3] Works for host and clients alike; late joiners immediately see the correct spray can colors.
[h3]Color Cycling[/h3] Scroll your mouse wheel while holding a spray can to cycle through all available paint colors. No more carrying twelve cans in a backpack.
[h3]Color Picking (Eyedropper)[/h3] Right-click any paintable object while holding a spray can to copy its color onto the can. Left-click the next item to match. [list] [*][b]Ctrl+right-click[/b] picks the as-built color the target would have coming out of its kit, independent of any later repaint [*][b]Shift+right-click[/b] is reserved (currently no-op) [*]Works on any paintable object [/list]
[h3]Infinite Spray Paint[/h3] All spray cans have unlimited uses and produce no pollution. Both are configurable.
[h3]Network Painting[/h3] Spray-paint a pipe, cable, chute, robotic arm rail, or elevator and the entire connected network gets painted at once. [list] [*][b]Hold Shift[/b] to paint just a single item (or swap this default, see Settings) [*][b]Hold Ctrl[/b] for a checkered/alternating paint pattern [*]Works on: pipe networks (including hydroponic trays and passive vents as separate paint groups), cable networks, chute networks, robotic arm rail assemblies (rails, junctions, bypass pieces, and docks), and elevators (all shaft and level segments; the carriage is painted separately) [/list]
[h3]Room & Structure Painting[/h3] Spray-paint a wall and every same-type wall bounding the same room is painted. Spray-paint a frame, girder, or any other large structure and all orthogonally-connected structures of the same exact type are painted with it. [list] [*]Walls use the game's Room membership to decide the paint set. Paint spills across any wall the room touches, but never past a doorway into another room [*]Large structures flood-fill on a grid using 6-neighbor (cardinal) adjacency only; diagonals are not followed [*]Same Shift / Ctrl modifiers apply [/list]
[h3]Glow Paint[/h3] The [b]Spray Paint Gun[/b] becomes a glow applicator: fire at a painted target to add a glow halo (visible in unlit rooms) while keeping its color. Every vanilla paint color supports glow. [list] [*]Gun is ammo-less. It no longer accepts spray cans; its can slot is hidden in the inventory [*]Gun never changes a target's color. To change color, paint with a plain can first; then fire the gun to add glow [*]Shift (single target) and Ctrl (checkered pattern) modifiers apply to gun-paint too [*]Right-click the gun to switch between [b]Add Glow[/b] and [b]Remove Glow[/b] modes (vanilla on/off toggle, HUD label rebranded) [*]Glow state persists across save/load and syncs correctly in multiplayer; every connected player sees the same glowing targets [*]Server toggle "Enable Glow Paint" lets admins disable the feature; when off, the gun reverts to the vanilla can-accepting behavior [/list]
[h3]Safe to Uninstall[/h3] Saves written by v1.6.0+ open cleanly without this mod; glow lives in a side-car the vanilla loader ignores. Re-install and load a pre-removal save to restore glow.
[h3]Settings[/h3]
All features are configurable via the mod settings panel.
[b]Client - Preferences[/b] (personal preference, each player sets independently): [list] [*][b]Paint Single Item By Default[/b] (default Off): Swap Shift behavior, single paint by default, hold Shift for network paint [*][b]Invert Color Scroll Direction[/b] (default Off): Reverse the scroll wheel direction [/list]
[b]Server - Consumables[/b] (the host's value controls gameplay for everyone): [list] [*][b]Unlimited Spray Paint Uses[/b] (default On): Infinite spray cans [*][b]Suppress Spray Paint Pollution[/b] (default On): No pollutant gas when spraying [/list]
[b]Server - Glow Paint[/b]: [list] [*][b]Enable Glow Paint[/b] (default On): The Spray Paint Gun adds glow to painted targets without changing color, and the gun no longer accepts spray cans. When off, the gun reverts to its vanilla behavior (accepts and uses loaded spray cans as ammo) [/list]
[b]Server - Network Painting[/b]: [list] [*][b]Enable Network Painting[/b] (default On): Paint entire networks at once [*][b]Network Paint Pipes[/b] (default On): Include pipes in network painting [*][b]Network Paint Cables[/b] (default On): Include cables in network painting [*][b]Network Paint Chutes[/b] (default On): Include chutes in network painting [*][b]Network Paint Walls[/b] (default On): Paint all same-type walls bounding the same room [*][b]Network Paint Rails[/b] (default On): Paint every rail, junction, bypass, and dock on the same robotic arm assembly in one stroke [*][b]Network Paint Large Structures[/b] (default On): Paint connected frames, girders, and other large structures in a 6-neighbor grid [*][b]Network Paint Elevators[/b] (default On): Paint all shaft and level segments on one elevator (the carriage is painted separately) [/list]
[h2]Compatibility[/h2]
[b]Requires:[/b] [list] [*][url=https://docs.bepinex.dev/]BepInEx[/url] [*][url=https://github.com/StationeersLaunchPad/StationeersLaunchPad]StationeersLaunchPad[/url] [/list]
[b]Incompatible with[/b] (detected at startup; the mod refuses to load if either is found): [list] [*][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3163662298]Color Cycler[/url] by Elmotrix [*][url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2876605527]Network Painter[/url] by Elmotrix [/list]
[b]Redundant[/b] (disable if installed): [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3576112002]Infinite Spray Paint[/url] by Aspct, [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=1761980496]Infinite Paint Mod[/url] by Dingo.
[b]All players[/b] (including dedicated servers) need the same BepInEx + StationeersLaunchPad + Spray Paint Plus setup; matching versions are enforced by the handshake.
[h2]Reporting Issues[/h2] If you run into a bug or something behaves unexpectedly, please open an issue on [url=https://github.com/SixFive7/StationeersPlus/issues]GitHub[/url]. Please include the mod name in the title so reports can be triaged. Steam comment notifications don't always come through, so GitHub is the reliable way to make sure a report is seen.
[h2]Credits[/h2]
Spray Paint Plus would not exist without the modders who came before: [list] [*][b]Elmotrix[/b]: Created [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3163662298]Color Cycler[/url] and [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2876605527]Network Painter[/url], the original spray paint enhancement mods for Stationeers. The core ideas of scroll-to-cycle and paint-entire-networks are theirs. [*][b]SubHobo[/b] (bls220): Contributed the initial multiplayer networking code to Color Cycler via [url=https://github.com/Elmotrix/ColorCyclerMod/pull/1]PR #1[/url]. [*][b]Aspct[/b]: Created [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3576112002]Infinite Spray Paint[/url], the original clean infinite paint mod for Stationeers. [*][b]Dingo (DingoPD)[/b]: Created the original [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=1761980496]Infinite Paint Mod[/url], the first infinite spray paint mod for Stationeers. [/list]
[url=https://github.com/SixFive7/StationeersPlus/tree/master/Mods/SprayPaintPlus]Source Code on GitHub[/url]
[h2]License[/h2] Licensed under the [url=https://github.com/SixFive7/StationeersPlus/blob/master/LICENSE]Apache License 2.0[/url]. Attribution required; see the LICENSE and NOTICE files at the repository root.
ModMetadata.ChangeLog: v1.8.0: Paint whole elevators - NEW: Painting any elevator shaft or level segment paints every shaft and level segment on the same elevator in one stroke, including the with-cable and without-cable build variants. - NEW: Server toggle "Network Paint Elevators" (default On). - The moving carriage is left out of the flood and painted on its own; painting the carriage directly does not repaint the shaft. - Shift restricts to the single targeted piece; Ctrl gives a checkered pattern that alternates segments up the column. - Multiplayer-safe; no save-format changes. - REQUIRES: All players on a server must run 1.8.0 (matching-version handshake).
ModMetadata.InGameDescription: Spray Paint Plus Combines color cycling, network painting, glow paint, and infinite spray paint into one multiplayer-safe Stationeers mod. Full multiplayer compatibility. Safe to remove from existing savegames. Features: - Scroll wheel cycles spray can colors - Right-click a target with a can to copy its color; Ctrl+right-click picks the as-built kit color - Infinite spray paint, no pollution (both configurable) - One-stroke paint for pipe / cable / chute networks, rooms of same-type walls, connected large structures, robotic arm rail assemblies, and elevators - Spray Paint Gun adds glow to painted targets (ammo-less, preserves color); right-click the gun to toggle Add Glow / Remove Glow mode - Shift = single item, Ctrl = checkered pattern - Full multiplayer with matching-version handshake - Safe to uninstall: v1.6.0+ saves open cleanly without this mod Settings: Server settings control gameplay for all players; client settings are personal. Requires: BepInEx + StationeersLaunchPad. All players, including dedicated servers, need the same setup. Incompatible with: Color Cycler, Network Painter (both by Elmotrix). Redundant (disable if installed): Infinite Spray Paint by Aspct, Infinite Paint Mod by Dingo. Credits: Elmotrix, SubHobo (bls220), Aspct, Dingo.
